display.purpose: Hero headline only, one per page.

title.purpose: Card and case-study titles; brightens to Signal Blue on hover.

label.purpose: Mono uppercase-tracked metadata: eyebrows, client names, status, content type, dates.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for all entrance and reveal animations site-wide.

motion.name: duration-reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll-triggered whileInView reveals, optionally staggered by idx * 100ms.

motion.name: duration-filter-slide
motion.purpose: Shared-element background sliding between active Work-page filter tabs.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action button. Ink fill, sharp corners, transitions to Signal Blue on hover/focus.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 16px 32px; background-color: #111111; color: #ffffff; font-weight: 500; border-radius: 0; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 0.3s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover, .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { background-color: #2563eb; outline: none; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #fafafa, 0 0 0 4px #2563eb; }

components.name: Tag
components.description: Static metadata chip used for tech/category tags. Never interactive.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-block; padding: 4px 12px; background-color: #0000000d; color: #111111cc; font-size: 0.75rem; border-radius: 0; }

components.name: Work Card
components.description: Image-and-title project card (List Card pattern). No border, no shadow, no radius — image scales on hover, title shifts to Signal Blue. Image is optional; text-only cards are valid.
components.html:
Project Title

Full-stack engineer
components.css: .ds-work-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 24px; text-decoration: none; } .ds-work-card-media { position: relative; aspect-ratio: 16 / 9; overflow: hidden; background-color: #f3f4f6; } .ds-work-card-media img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; transition: transform 1s; } .ds-work-card:hover .ds-work-card-media img { transform: scale(1.05); } .ds-work-card-title { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #111111; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-work-card:hover .ds-work-card-title { color: #2563eb; } .ds-work-card-role { color: #6b7280; }

components.name: Framed Card
components.description: Bordered white-surface card for heterogeneous grid items (Playground) — the system's second card pattern, distinct from the borderless List Card.
components.html:
◆ INTERACTIVE

Experiment Title

One line describing the experiment.

CanvasWebGL
components.css: .ds-framed-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; padding: 24px; background-color: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #0000000d; border-radius: 0; text-decoration: none; transition: border-color 0.3s; } .ds-framed-card:hover { border-color: #00000026; } .ds-framed-card-type { font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #9ca3af; } .ds-framed-card-title { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 500; color: #111111; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-framed-card:hover .ds-framed-card-title { color: #2563eb; } .ds-framed-card-desc { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #4b5563; } .ds-framed-card-tags { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; margin-top: auto; padding-top: 8px; }

components.name: Editorial Split Row
components.description: Signature 12-column layout: narrow metadata rail (client/date) on the left, wide content column on the right.
components.html:
CLIENT NAME

6 weeks

Case study title

One or two sentences describing the engagement and outcome.

AstroReact
components.css: .ds-split-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr); gap: 32px; } .ds-split-meta { grid-column: span 4; } .ds-split-client { font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6b7280; } .ds-split-date { color: #9ca3af; margin-top: 4px; } .ds-split-content { grid-column: span 8; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 24px; } .ds-split-title { font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #111111; transition: color 0.5s; } .ds-split-row:hover .ds-split-title { color: #2563eb; } .ds-split-desc { font-size: 1.125rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #4b5563; max-width: 60ch; } .ds-split-tags { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; margin-top: 16px; }

components.name: Navigation
components.description: Fixed, translucent-blur header with wordmark, flat desktop link row, and mobile hamburger toggle. The system's one deliberately non-flat surface.
components.html:
Vinayak Gupta
WorkNowAbout
components.css: .ds-nav { position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 50; background-color: #fafafacc; backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border-bottom: 1px solid #0000000d; } .ds-nav-inner { max-width: 80rem; margin: 0 auto; height: 64px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 0 24px; } .ds-nav-wordmark { font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #111111; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-nav-wordmark:hover { color: #2563eb; } .ds-nav-links { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 32px; } .ds-nav-link { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #4b5563; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #111111; }

components.name: Filter Tab
components.description: Flat text-button filter row with a shared-element (layoutId) background that slides to the active tab. Used on /work for category filtering.
components.css: .ds-filter-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; } .ds-filter-tab { position: relative; padding: 8px 16px; background: none; border: none; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #9ca3af; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-filter-tab:hover { color: #6b7280; } .ds-filter-tab-active { color: #111111; background-color: #0000000d; }

narrative.overview:
This site reads like annotations in a lab notebook, not a marketing brochure: monochrome type carrying nearly all the weight, a single utilitarian accent reserved for signal (links, primary actions, hover states), and structure built from whitespace and hairline rules rather than cards or shadows. Nothing is decorated to look impressive — it's arranged to be read quickly and trusted immediately, the same way a well-kept notebook earns trust through legibility, not polish.

The system explicitly rejects the consultant sales-deck register it's evolving away from (stats-grid "proof" sections, "Book a call" CTAs, service-tier offerings), the generic SaaS/AI aesthetic (purple gradients, glassmorphism, hero-metric templates), and the stiff formality of a corporate resume PDF. It is warm through voice and content, not through visual softness — the palette and shapes stay plain so the writing can carry the personality.

Now implemented across six pages (/, /work, /now, /playground + detail routes, /about, /resume), all sharing this system through the persistent Nav and Footer.
keyCharacteristics: Monochrome base (off-white / near-black) with one electric-blue signal color used sparingly
Zero border-radius anywhere, with one narrow accepted exception for circular wayfinding dots on the /about timeline
Flat by default — no shadows at rest; separation comes from whitespace and 1px hairline dividers
Generous vertical rhythm (96–128px between sections) against tight, editorial line lengths
Motion is a confirmation, not a performance: fades and small upward translations, nothing bouncy
A persistent, translucent-blur navigation header sits above every page — the one deliberate break from the flat, opaque default, justified by needing to stay legible over scrolling content

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue appears in exactly one state at a time — a hover, a focus ring, a selection highlight. It never sits on the page at rest, with one narrow exception: the /about timeline's wayfinding dots, which use blue as a permanent location mark, not decoration. That exception doesn't generalize.

rules.name: The One Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: Uppercase-tracked mono labels are reserved for genuine metadata (a client name, a status, a content type), never used as decorative section kickers. A status can also carry a single leading emoji (⚡🔭💤) as the system's one icon language for status — not a general invitation to decorate headings with emoji.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: If it's part of the page's normal layout, it has no shadow, no radius, and no blur. Those are earned only by content that overlays the page in a real stacking sense — the fixed nav, a modal, a menu, a tooltip — never a card, button, or section in normal flow.

dos: Do keep every corner sharp — zero border-radius anywhere, except the /about timeline's small circular wayfinding dots.
Do hold Signal Blue (#2563eb) in reserve for exactly one active state at a time — hover, focus, or selection — never at rest, except the timeline's permanent wayfinding dots.
Do use the 1px Hairline (#0000001a) or its lighter sibling Hairline Soft (#0000000d) as the section/list-separation device; prefer whitespace over any new visual boundary.
Do cap body text between 25ch and 65ch depending on column width, and keep the mono Label style exclusive to genuine metadata.
Do let motion read as confirmation (fade + small upward translation, ease [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1]) — never bounce, elastic, or overshoot.
Do reserve backdrop-blur for the fixed nav specifically — it's functional, not a decorative material to reach for elsewhere.
donts: Don't reintroduce the consultant sales-deck register — no stats-grid "proof" sections, no "Book a call" CTA, no service-tier offering lists.
Don't use purple/multi-color gradients, glassmorphism as decoration, or a hero-metric template (big number + small label + gradient accent) — the generic SaaS/AI look this system explicitly rejects.
Don't let the site read like a stiff, formal resume PDF — copy stays first-person and conversational even where structure is dense.

Don't add a drop shadow or colored border-stripe to any card, button, or list item sitting in normal page flow — reserve shadow/blur for genuinely overlaid surfaces only (the fixed nav, modals, dropdowns, tooltips).
Don't apply an uppercase mono eyebrow to every section head as decoration — it's reserved for sections with real label-shaped metadata (client, status, date, content type).
Don't use the Framed Card's white surface and border as a substitute for the plain List Card pattern — it's for grids of heterogeneous items and framed external content, not a general card upgrade.
